topic: Someone discovers their recipe has become famous under another name
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara stared at the glossy magazine spread, the headline screaming the name "The Golden Crumb." Her hands, usually steady when kneading dough, were trembling slightly as she traced the elegant script of the recipe. It was hers. Her grandmother’s secret sourdough starter, the precise hydration levels, the slow, patient fermentation—it was all written down here, attributed to a culinary sensation that wasn't hers. A cold knot tightened in her stomach. She reread the ingredient list, the familiar scent of yeast and warm flour suddenly feeling tainted, like a borrowed perfume. She pushed the magazine away, the sound of the paper rustling loud in the quiet kitchen. "No," she whispered, the sound thin and brittle. She started scrubbing down the counter, her movements sharp and jerky, trying to erase the phantom echo of someone else's triumph.

[story 2]

Mark felt the air leave his lungs in a sharp gasp. The online comments section was a tidal wave of adoration, all directed at a dish he had only ever made for his own family. "This is the best thing I've ever tasted!" "The secret is out!" The words swam before his eyes, blurring the meticulously organized notes he’d kept hidden in his journal. He picked up his phone, his thumb hovering over the screen, unable to click on the viral video showcasing his simple weeknight pasta. A flush crept up his neck, hot and undeniable. He paced the small apartment, his footsteps echoing hollowly on the hardwood floor. He stopped by the pantry, his fingers brushing against the labeled jar of heirloom tomatoes, suddenly seeing them not as humble produce, but as the foundation of a global phenomenon. He leaned against the cool metal of the refrigerator, breathing deeply, trying to anchor himself against the sudden, dizzying shift in reality.

[story 3]

For years, Liam had cherished the ritual of baking. Every morning, the precise measurements, the quiet concentration, the resulting loaf that always tasted like home. Then, the email arrived: a feature in a major food publication, showcasing "The Artisan’s Secret." The accompanying photo was of a loaf identical to his own, displayed on a pedestal in a sleek, unfamiliar setting. Liam sat at his kitchen table, the parchment paper beneath his fingertips suddenly feeling flimsy and inadequate. He picked up his phone, scrolling through the articles, the praise washing over him like a sudden, unexpected wave. A strange hollowness settled in his chest. He looked down at his hands, noticing the faint flour dusting his knuckles. He tried to focus on the texture of the wooden cutting board, but his gaze kept drifting to the screen, a profound sense of displacement washing over him. He slowly set the phone down, the silence of the room amplifying the frantic thumping in his own ears.
